instruction_completeness_test.reasoning: Reading only instruction.md reveals success criteria but not the active-vs-durable authority split, replay mutation point, or emission trust boundary; source and runtime engagement remain necessary.

discovery_budget.discovery: Which local authority should determine later-generation workload records after restore and replay.
discovery_budget.why_instruction_must_not_reveal: Naming the authority would reveal the diagnosis and collapse the task into wiring one named source into reports.

discovery_budget.discovery: How replay boundaries mutate durable generation data during cleanup windows.
discovery_budget.why_instruction_must_not_reveal: The public prompt may describe post-replay drift, but exact phase ordering and mutation source are the repair path.

discovery_budget.discovery: Why reconstructed summaries can look healthy while canonical artifacts diverge later.
discovery_budget.why_instruction_must_not_reveal: Telling solvers which artifact is over-trusted would turn the task into replacing one input to the emitter.

discovery_budget.discovery: Which rerun/idempotence obligations survive output deletion and repeated recovery.
discovery_budget.why_instruction_must_not_reveal: The instruction can require regenerated output and repeatability, but not which intermediate cache must be rebuilt.

discovery_budget.discovery: Which negative controls must remain unchanged while fixing drift.
discovery_budget.why_instruction_must_not_reveal: The controls should be discovered from local scenarios so the solver does not simply apply a broad state reset.

instruction_specificity.reasoning: The planned instruction states observed recovery drift, command/output/schema, regeneration, and durability expectations without naming shadow metadata, active state, canonical artifact selection, or replay-order fix.

attack_path.summary: A shortcut solver may try to hardcode the JSON report, patch only the runner, or trust the first health row.
attack_path.reasoning: The verifier deletes and regenerates output, uses multiple scenarios and reruns, and derives invariants from traces so surface health and static reports are insufficient.

smallest_plausible_patch.summary: A valid minimal repair coordinates authority selection, replay/cleanup mutation handling, durable artifact reads, and observation framing across four files.
smallest_plausible_patch.reasoning: A one-line source priority change, cache deletion, or summary-to-artifact swap alone fails at least one planned subset: cleanup idempotence, later-generation continuity, negative controls, or regenerated reports.

collapse_audit.reasoning: Stage: draft. Smallest plausible successful patch is a coordinated four-frontier recovery repair, likely editable frontier spans core ledger, operation tape, storage, and emission. Requirement-to-file mapping is intentionally many-to-many: recovery convergence and drift checks require multiple layers. Oracle LOC is not yet available but the spec requires substantive multi-file logic and CR2/GX3 guardrails. Discoverability remains fair because public surfaces expose symptoms and contracts but not the internal authority split or replay phase.
collapse_audit.residual_hardness: After full disclosure of schema, command, and recovery invariants, the solver must still infer authority, phase, durability, and emission interactions from local code and regenerated traces.

topology_enumeration.summary: Repair by canonicalizing authority selection before replay output is framed.
topology_enumeration.why_no_single_location_suffices: Authority selection without replay and emission changes leaves stale summaries or broken reruns; emission-only changes leave durable drift.

topology_enumeration.summary: Repair by making replay/cleanup produce idempotent durable generations and updating readers to consume them.
topology_enumeration.why_no_single_location_suffices: Cleanup-only fixes do not align output framing or negative controls, and storage-only fixes do not handle replay ordering.

topology_enumeration.summary: Repair by rebuilding observation generation around artifact provenance while preserving runtime continuity.
topology_enumeration.why_no_single_location_suffices: Observation-only repair cannot guarantee canonical state; main-only repair cannot pass provenance and idempotence checks.

symbol_table.signature: def fold_units(items: list[object], mark: str | None = None) -> dict[str, object]
symbol_table.purpose: Combines collected records into the intermediate map used by the harness.

symbol_table.signature: def advance_span(batch: list[object], marker: str | None = None) -> list[dict[str, object]]
symbol_table.purpose: Builds the next operation sequence from a batch of local records.

symbol_table.signature: def bind_entry(slot: str, payload: dict[str, object]) -> dict[str, object]
symbol_table.purpose: Loads and stores structured local entries used by the runner.

symbol_table.signature: def shape_rows(label: str, frame: dict[str, object], order: list[str]) -> list[dict[str, object]]
symbol_table.purpose: Converts intermediate maps into externally visible observation rows.

decoy_manifest.non_fix_purpose: Writes diagnostic snapshots for operator inspection outside verifier-facing observations.
Performs sanity checks for local fixture rows before execution begins.
Prints operator-facing dry-run traces for manual inspection.

renames_during_drafting.reason: path token overlapped with instruction noun migration/state and would increase grep-collapse risk.
symbol overlapped with instruction noun replay and revealed the phase under diagnosis.
test name overlapped with instruction nouns replay and cleanup.
symbol overlapped with instruction noun observation.

difficulty_mechanism_plan.placement: runner scenarios perform restore, replay, cleanup, deletion, and rerun sequences before observations are stable.
difficulty_mechanism_plan.why_model_misses_it: A model can stop at the first healthy run and miss drift that appears only after later sequences.
difficulty_mechanism_plan.fairness_guardrail: All sequences are deterministic and reproducible through the public command and local fixtures.

difficulty_mechanism_plan.placement: initial health records and reconstructed summaries look internally consistent while later records expose divergence.
difficulty_mechanism_plan.why_model_misses_it: Surface checks encourage premature closure around the wrong authority.
difficulty_mechanism_plan.fairness_guardrail: The final verifier derives invariants from full observation traces, not hidden facts.

difficulty_mechanism_plan.placement: cleanup and rerun behavior must be safe after output deletion and repeated recovery.
difficulty_mechanism_plan.why_model_misses_it: Narrow fixes often pass one replay but fail idempotence or negative controls.
difficulty_mechanism_plan.fairness_guardrail: The public prompt names idempotent recovery and regeneration requirements.

difficulty_mechanism_plan.placement: Python source, JSON fixtures, visible docs, emitted observations, and construction manifest must agree.
difficulty_mechanism_plan.why_model_misses_it: Single-file fixes leave fixture semantics, docs, or report framing inconsistent.
difficulty_mechanism_plan.fairness_guardrail: Every externally tested schema and artifact rule has a visible home.

difficulty_mechanism_plan.placement: the solver must run and compare generated traces across modes to isolate the durable drift.
difficulty_mechanism_plan.why_model_misses_it: Static inspection alone may identify several plausible authorities that all look locally consistent.
difficulty_mechanism_plan.fairness_guardrail: No timing, latency, network, or nondeterministic crash injection is required.

calibration_plan.human_sanity: One careful human can solve using bundled source, fixtures, docs, public command, and regenerated observations in a few hours.
calibration_plan.shortcut_audit: Try static JSON writes, runner-only patches, broad state clearing, summary-only trust, test edits/deletions, and direct reward writes.
calibration_plan.ablation_plan: Remove one layer at a time: deceptive first health, cleanup rerun, durable artifact provenance, and cross-format schema checks; each removal should make a distinct shortcut viable.

pass_rate_target.basis: stronger target-agent trials on the finalized single-container task.

metrics.criterion: regenerated observations satisfy all recovery, replay, cleanup, and convergence checks.
derived provenance, generation continuity, digest, and idempotence invariants hold across scenarios.
reruns after deletion and cleanup are deterministic and do not leave stale mutable state.
public command, output path, JSON schema, and local-only runtime contract remain intact.
all required files and executable scripts are present with no forbidden packaged artifacts.

verifier_scoring_plan.binary_threshold_rule: reward.txt is 1 only when every semantic and static verifier check passes; partial metric weights are for author calibration.

subtype_milestone_plan.sequential_dependency: single-step task; sequential dependency is encoded inside deterministic recovery scenarios rather than milestone folders.
subtype_milestone_plan.sidecar_or_protocol_notes: all sidecar-like inputs are local JSON/docs fixtures under environment; no external service, database, UI, or runtime web access.

task_files.role: local input scenarios and negative controls
visible product documentation for schema and recovery guarantees

fix_frontier.distribution: one core combiner, one operation sequencer, one durable entry handler, and one observation emitter across distinct package roots; no location controls more than 25% of tests.
fix_frontier.naming_policy: Fix-path symbols use neutral engineering names and avoid every extracted instruction noun; tests use neutral numeric IDs.
fix_frontier.helpers_policy: Co-resident helpers and decoys perform genuine diagnostics or sanity checks; no cosmetic decoys or detector-padding files.

forbidden_assertion_styles: tests that only check JSON shape

task_shape.hardness_source: diagnosis plus coordinated repair across local recovery state and regenerated artifacts
task_shape.collapse_risk: naming the restore boundary or trusted artifact too directly could reduce the task to replacing one source in one emitter.

category_profile.challenge_family: live recovery replay boundary drift with durable artifact authority mismatch
category_profile.bug_family: live recovery replay boundary drift with durable artifact authority mismatch
category_profile.allowed_instruction_disclosures: Crash/restart or recovery workflow, durability guarantee, recovery command, regenerated output path, report schema, externally visible post-recovery state, and idempotent rerun expectations.
category_profile.forbidden_instruction_leaks: Journal/checkpoint internals, shadow-vs-active authority mismatch, broken recovery phase, corrupt record, replay function, cleanup path, exact phase ordering, or patch location.
category_profile.category_specific_hardness_bar: Partial writes, replay, cleanup, idempotence, and rerun safety must coordinate across generated observations and durable local artifacts.
category_profile.category_specific_verifier_risks: Nondeterministic crash timing, one hidden snapshot, verifier trusting private internals, static report writes, and special-casing one scenario.
category_profile.coverage_role: Adds a state recovery crash-consistency profile task using migration-style replay drift, complementing the promoted config-policy reference rather than duplicating it.

satisfiability_risk.rc2_planned_name_risk: low; fix-path paths use app/core/ops/io/emit and neutral symbols while instruction nouns are banned from symbols and test names.
satisfiability_risk.gx9_contract_risk: low; the output is observation-shaped and tests derive invariants from traces rather than instruction scenario-field-value tables.
satisfiability_risk.cr1_symbol_frontier_risk: low; the construction manifest predeclares four symbol-thin frontier functions across distinct roots.
satisfiability_risk.hidden_contract_risk: medium but controlled; schema, command, regenerated output rule, digest rule, and recovery guarantees must be mirrored in instruction.md or visible environment docs.

actionability_plan.verifier_command_visible: instruction.md will name `python3 /app/environment/runner.py --write /app/output/migration_observations.json` as the normal regeneration command.
actionability_plan.source_fix_intent_visible: instruction.md will state that source under `/app/environment` must be fixed so normal regeneration produces correct observations; static output writes are insufficient.
actionability_plan.generated_output_rule_visible: instruction.md will state tests delete `/app/output/migration_observations.json`, rerun the harness, and inspect the regenerated records.
actionability_plan.exact_formula_home: instruction.md and visible environment docs will define only product-level derived checks: stable record digest from ordered observations and idempotent equality across reruns; implementation formula homes stay in docs/source, not tests alone.
actionability_plan.schema_home: instruction.md and environment/app/docs/contract.md will define `runs`, `steps`, `records`, `artifacts`, and per-record fields such as `name`, `generation`, `owner`, `lineage`, and `evidence`.

waiver_plan.waiver_rationale: No waivers planned; naming, output-contract shape, and multi-frontier construction address expected collapse and hidden-contract pressure.

realism_source.evidence_basis: Synthesized goal-sized survivor based on real classes of live migration, checkpoint replay, and recovery-controller bugs where a superficially healthy restored workload later diverges from canonical durable state.
realism_source.upstream_or_synthetic_rationale: No specific upstream bug is being cited; the synthetic exception is justified because the minimized local harness preserves a real workflow: restore, replay, cleanup, regenerated evidence, and post-recovery validation without external infrastructure.
realism_source.minimization_preserves: The design preserves false-green surface validation, multiple plausible authorities, replay-boundary mutation, cleanup windows, durable artifacts, idempotent reruns, and generated observation reports.
realism_source.synthetic_exception_review: Required and satisfied at spec level: no invented answer-key booleans, no metaphor-only prompt, local-only deterministic data, explicit actionability plan, no planned waivers, and extra collapse scrutiny via four-frontier flipping contract.

hardness_axes.name: Discover
hardness_axes.reasoning: The public prompt can expose recovery symptoms, command, output path, schema, and durability guarantees, but the solver must discover which in-memory and durable authorities disagree by reading source, fixtures, and regenerated traces.

hardness_axes.name: Synthesize
hardness_axes.reasoning: A valid repair spans checkpoint input material, replay sequencing, cleanup handling, active runtime records, and report emission; no single module or artifact contains the full solution.

hardness_axes.name: Navigate coupling
hardness_axes.reasoning: Local changes to replay order, durable metadata, or report framing can each break idempotence, ownership continuity, cleanup safety, or regenerated output consistency.

hardness_axes.name: Reason beyond training
hardness_axes.reasoning: The work is not a textbook checkpoint or JSON validation recipe; it requires reasoning about local authority selection, generation boundaries, replay idempotence, and evidence-rich recovery observations.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Disclosure-collapse
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: After all tested recovery guarantees and schema fields are disclosed, the solver still must diagnose which local authority and replay boundary corrupts later-generation observations.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Hidden-instance
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Difficulty is not finding one corrupt row; tests must exercise multiple generations, cleanup paths, reruns, and independent bundles.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Single-artifact repair
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The planned flipping contract requires at least four locations across core, operations, storage, and emission; copying or editing one manifest cannot satisfy all checks.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Generalization
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Verifier scenarios cover clean restore, replay after cleanup, repeated rerun, divergent records, and negative controls rather than one named broken case.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Prompt-honesty
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: An honest prompt can state the recovery command, output schema, and observable drift without naming shadow metadata, active-state selection, or exact replay phase.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Cheating-vs-difficulty
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Anti-cheating controls such as deleting output are not treated as hardness; the hard work is source diagnosis across recovery state and generated observations.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Mechanical-fix filter
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The seed is not about verifier dependencies, timeout tuning, or reward formatting; it is about durable recovery semantics.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Localized-fix
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The spec requires no one obvious handler and no public patch target; planned tests fail meaningful subsets when any one frontier location is reverted.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Oracle-locality
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The oracle must coordinate source changes across four behavioral roots rather than replace one or two files wholesale.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Small declarative-cluster
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The solution is not a small table: replay, cleanup, authority choice, durable artifact writing, and observation framing must agree.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Grep-collapse
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Instruction nouns are banned from fix-path symbols and planned test names use neutral IDs, so grep does not directly land on the patch.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Pre-factored-helper
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Fix-path symbols are planned as neutral local engineering names whose names do not mirror public symptoms or requirements.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Recipe-discount
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The task is not a standard safe-extraction, manifest regeneration, or finite state-table recipe; semantic authority has to be inferred from local behavior.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Security-aura discount
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: No security flavor is used as a substitute for hardness; the difficulty is recovery/reconciliation reasoning.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Orthogonal-checklist
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The checks are coupled: replay changes affect cleanup idempotence, durable artifacts, later-generation records, and emitted observations.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Harness-discount
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Docker and runner setup are ordinary single-container scaffolding and are not counted as difficulty.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: One-pass solvability
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: A frontier model opening obvious files would still need to run the pipeline and compare generation traces to identify the real authority split and safe fix.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Hard-only gate
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The residual task remains hard after disclosure because it requires multi-surface recovery diagnosis and coordinated repair, not medium-scale implementation.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Instruction specificity test
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: For repair_existing_system the instruction will be symptoms-only, not cause-revealing or spec-complete.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Topology distribution test
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Three repair topologies are viable and each requires at least three coordinated layers; no single component can pass the planned verifier alone.

rubric_axes.name: Verifiable
rubric_axes.reasoning: Deterministic local commands regenerate a JSON observation artifact and tests derive invariant checks from records, digests, and reruns.

rubric_axes.name: Well-specified
rubric_axes.reasoning: The public contract can be short and concrete: command, output path, schema, source-fix requirement, and observable recovery guarantees.

rubric_axes.name: Solvable
rubric_axes.reasoning: An expert can solve in a few hours by inspecting the local harness, source, fixtures, and test failures; the codebase remains small and single-container.

rubric_axes.name: Difficult
rubric_axes.reasoning: The planned task demands professional debugging of replay state, durable artifacts, authority selection, and idempotence under misleading first-pass health.

rubric_axes.name: Interesting
rubric_axes.reasoning: Live migration and recovery drift is economically meaningful because false health checks can promote stale or cross-generation workloads.

rubric_axes.name: Outcome-verified
rubric_axes.reasoning: Tests grade regenerated artifacts and behavioral invariants, not process choices or a required implementation approach.

instruction_completeness_test.reasoning: Reading only instruction.md reveals success criteria but not the active-vs-durable authority split, replay mutation point, or emission trust boundary; source and runtime engagement remain necessary.

category_profile.category_specific_verifier_risks: Nondeterministic crash timing, one hidden snapshot, verifier trusting private internals, static report writes, and special-casing one scenario.
category_profile.coverage_role: Adds a state recovery crash-consistency profile task using migration-style replay drift, complementing the promoted config-policy reference rather than duplicating it.

satisfiability_risk.rc2_planned_name_risk: low; fix-path paths use app/core/ops/io/emit and neutral symbols while instruction nouns are banned from symbols and test names.
satisfiability_risk.gx9_contract_risk: low; the output is observation-shaped and tests derive invariants from traces rather than instruction scenario-field-value tables.
satisfiability_risk.cr1_symbol_frontier_risk: low; the construction manifest predeclares four symbol-thin frontier functions across distinct roots.
satisfiability_risk.hidden_contract_risk: medium but controlled; schema, command, regenerated output rule, digest rule, and recovery guarantees must be mirrored in instruction.md or visible environment docs.

actionability_plan.verifier_command_visible: instruction.md will name `python3 /app/environment/runner.py --write /app/output/migration_observations.json` as the normal regeneration command.
actionability_plan.source_fix_intent_visible: instruction.md will state that source under `/app/environment` must be fixed so normal regeneration produces correct observations; static output writes are insufficient.
actionability_plan.generated_output_rule_visible: instruction.md will state tests delete `/app/output/migration_observations.json`, rerun the harness, and inspect the regenerated records.
actionability_plan.exact_formula_home: instruction.md and visible environment docs will define only product-level derived checks: stable record digest from ordered observations and idempotent equality across reruns; implementation formula homes stay in docs/source, not tests alone.
actionability_plan.schema_home: instruction.md and environment/app/docs/contract.md will define `runs`, `steps`, `records`, `artifacts`, and per-record fields such as `name`, `generation`, `owner`, `lineage`, and `evidence`.

waiver_plan.waiver_rationale: No waivers planned; naming, output-contract shape, and multi-frontier construction address expected collapse and hidden-contract pressure.

realism_source.evidence_basis: Synthesized goal-sized survivor based on real classes of live migration, checkpoint replay, and recovery-controller bugs where a superficially healthy restored workload later diverges from canonical durable state.
realism_source.upstream_or_synthetic_rationale: No specific upstream bug is being cited; the synthetic exception is justified because the minimized local harness preserves a real workflow: restore, replay, cleanup, regenerated evidence, and post-recovery validation without external infrastructure.
realism_source.minimization_preserves: The design preserves false-green surface validation, multiple plausible authorities, replay-boundary mutation, cleanup windows, durable artifacts, idempotent reruns, and generated observation reports.
realism_source.synthetic_exception_review: Required and satisfied at spec level: no invented answer-key booleans, no metaphor-only prompt, local-only deterministic data, explicit actionability plan, no planned waivers, and extra collapse scrutiny via four-frontier flipping contract.

evidence_errors: hardness_axes entry 'diagnose_or_design' must use name 'Diagnose or Design/Search', found 'Diagnose'.
anti_trivialization_checks entry 'discovery_budget_test' must use name 'Discovery/design budget test', found 'Discovery / design budget test'.
blocking_failures: hardness_axes entry 'diagnose_or_design' must use name 'Diagnose or Design/Search', found 'Diagnose'.
anti_trivialization_checks entry 'discovery_budget_test' must use name 'Discovery/design budget test', found 'Discovery / design budget test'.

hardness_axes.name: Discover
hardness_axes.reasoning: The public prompt can expose recovery symptoms, command, output path, schema, and durability guarantees, but the solver must discover which in-memory and durable authorities disagree by reading source, fixtures, and regenerated traces.

hardness_axes.name: Synthesize
hardness_axes.reasoning: A valid repair spans checkpoint input material, replay sequencing, cleanup handling, active runtime records, and report emission; no single module or artifact contains the full solution.

hardness_axes.name: Diagnose or Design/Search
hardness_axes.reasoning: The task is repair_existing_system and the planned instruction remains symptoms-only: recovered workloads initially look healthy, then later generations reveal drift after replay and cleanup sequences.

hardness_axes.name: Navigate coupling
hardness_axes.reasoning: Local changes to replay order, durable metadata, or report framing can each break idempotence, ownership continuity, cleanup safety, or regenerated output consistency.

hardness_axes.name: Reason beyond training
hardness_axes.reasoning: The work is not a textbook checkpoint or JSON validation recipe; it requires reasoning about local authority selection, generation boundaries, replay idempotence, and evidence-rich recovery observations.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Disclosure-collapse
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: After all tested recovery guarantees and schema fields are disclosed, the solver still must diagnose which local authority and replay boundary corrupts later-generation observations.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Hidden-instance
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Difficulty is not finding one corrupt row; tests must exercise multiple generations, cleanup paths, reruns, and independent bundles.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Single-artifact repair
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The planned flipping contract requires at least four locations across core, operations, storage, and emission; copying or editing one manifest cannot satisfy all checks.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Generalization
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Verifier scenarios cover clean restore, replay after cleanup, repeated rerun, divergent records, and negative controls rather than one named broken case.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Prompt-honesty
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: An honest prompt can state the recovery command, output schema, and observable drift without naming shadow metadata, active-state selection, or exact replay phase.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Cheating-vs-difficulty
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Anti-cheating controls such as deleting output are not treated as hardness; the hard work is source diagnosis across recovery state and generated observations.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Mechanical-fix filter
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The seed is not about verifier dependencies, timeout tuning, or reward formatting; it is about durable recovery semantics.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Localized-fix
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The spec requires no one obvious handler and no public patch target; planned tests fail meaningful subsets when any one frontier location is reverted.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Oracle-locality
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The oracle must coordinate source changes across four behavioral roots rather than replace one or two files wholesale.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Small declarative-cluster
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The solution is not a small table: replay, cleanup, authority choice, durable artifact writing, and observation framing must agree.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Grep-collapse
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Instruction nouns are banned from fix-path symbols and planned test names use neutral IDs, so grep does not directly land on the patch.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Pre-factored-helper
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Fix-path symbols are planned as neutral local engineering names whose names do not mirror public symptoms or requirements.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Recipe-discount
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The task is not a standard safe-extraction, manifest regeneration, or finite state-table recipe; semantic authority has to be inferred from local behavior.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Security-aura discount
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: No security flavor is used as a substitute for hardness; the difficulty is recovery/reconciliation reasoning.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Orthogonal-checklist
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The checks are coupled: replay changes affect cleanup idempotence, durable artifacts, later-generation records, and emitted observations.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Harness-discount
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Docker and runner setup are ordinary single-container scaffolding and are not counted as difficulty.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: One-pass solvability
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: A frontier model opening obvious files would still need to run the pipeline and compare generation traces to identify the real authority split and safe fix.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Hard-only gate
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The residual task remains hard after disclosure because it requires multi-surface recovery diagnosis and coordinated repair, not medium-scale implementation.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Discovery/design budget test
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: At least five non-trivial discoveries are planned: authority mismatch, replay boundary mutation, cleanup durability, summary trust, and idempotent rerun behavior.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Instruction specificity test
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: For repair_existing_system the instruction will be symptoms-only, not cause-revealing or spec-complete.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Topology distribution test
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Three repair topologies are viable and each requires at least three coordinated layers; no single component can pass the planned verifier alone.

rubric_axes.name: Verifiable
rubric_axes.reasoning: Deterministic local commands regenerate a JSON observation artifact and tests derive invariant checks from records, digests, and reruns.

rubric_axes.name: Well-specified
rubric_axes.reasoning: The public contract can be short and concrete: command, output path, schema, source-fix requirement, and observable recovery guarantees.

rubric_axes.name: Solvable
rubric_axes.reasoning: An expert can solve in a few hours by inspecting the local harness, source, fixtures, and test failures; the codebase remains small and single-container.

rubric_axes.name: Difficult
rubric_axes.reasoning: The planned task demands professional debugging of replay state, durable artifacts, authority selection, and idempotence under misleading first-pass health.

rubric_axes.name: Interesting
rubric_axes.reasoning: Live migration and recovery drift is economically meaningful because false health checks can promote stale or cross-generation workloads.

rubric_axes.name: Outcome-verified
rubric_axes.reasoning: Tests grade regenerated artifacts and behavioral invariants, not process choices or a required implementation approach.

instruction_completeness_test.reasoning: Reading only instruction.md reveals success criteria but not the active-vs-durable authority split, replay mutation point, or emission trust boundary; source and runtime engagement remain necessary.

discovery_budget.discovery: Which local authority should determine later-generation workload records after restore and replay.
discovery_budget.why_instruction_must_not_reveal: Naming the authority would reveal the diagnosis and collapse the task into wiring one named source into reports.

discovery_budget.discovery: How replay boundaries mutate durable generation data during cleanup windows.
discovery_budget.why_instruction_must_not_reveal: The public prompt may describe post-replay drift, but exact phase ordering and mutation source are the repair path.

discovery_budget.discovery: Why reconstructed summaries can look healthy while canonical artifacts diverge later.
discovery_budget.why_instruction_must_not_reveal: Telling solvers which artifact is over-trusted would turn the task into replacing one input to the emitter.

discovery_budget.discovery: Which rerun/idempotence obligations survive output deletion and repeated recovery.
discovery_budget.why_instruction_must_not_reveal: The instruction can require regenerated output and repeatability, but not which intermediate cache must be rebuilt.

discovery_budget.discovery: Which negative controls must remain unchanged while fixing drift.
discovery_budget.why_instruction_must_not_reveal: The controls should be discovered from local scenarios so the solver does not simply apply a broad state reset.

instruction_specificity.reasoning: The planned instruction states observed recovery drift, command/output/schema, regeneration, and durability expectations without naming shadow metadata, active state, canonical artifact selection, or replay-order fix.

attack_path.summary: A shortcut solver may try to hardcode the JSON report, patch only the runner, or trust the first health row.
attack_path.reasoning: The verifier deletes and regenerates output, uses multiple scenarios and reruns, and derives invariants from traces so surface health and static reports are insufficient.

smallest_plausible_patch.summary: A valid minimal repair coordinates authority selection, replay/cleanup mutation handling, durable artifact reads, and observation framing across four files.
smallest_plausible_patch.reasoning: A one-line source priority change, cache deletion, or summary-to-artifact swap alone fails at least one planned subset: cleanup idempotence, later-generation continuity, negative controls, or regenerated reports.

collapse_audit.reasoning: Attempt 3 structural correction: fixed the two remaining canonical label mismatches for diagnose_or_design and discovery_budget_test; no task-design change beyond evidence-contract compliance. Attempt 2 structural correction: the prior record failed because evidence used numbered IDs (H1/AT1/R1) instead of canonical schema IDs; this attempt preserves the same design and changes only evidence identifiers to the required names. Stage: draft. Smallest plausible successful patch is a coordinated four-frontier recovery repair, likely editable frontier spans core ledger, operation tape, storage, and emission. Requirement-to-file mapping is intentionally many-to-many: recovery convergence and drift checks require multiple layers. Oracle LOC is not yet available but the spec requires substantive multi-file logic and CR2/GX3 guardrails. Discoverability remains fair because public surfaces expose symptoms and contracts but not the internal authority split or replay phase.
collapse_audit.residual_hardness: After full disclosure of schema, command, and recovery invariants, the solver must still infer authority, phase, durability, and emission interactions from local code and regenerated traces.

topology_enumeration.summary: Repair by canonicalizing authority selection before replay output is framed.
topology_enumeration.why_no_single_location_suffices: Authority selection without replay and emission changes leaves stale summaries or broken reruns; emission-only changes leave durable drift.

topology_enumeration.summary: Repair by making replay/cleanup produce idempotent durable generations and updating readers to consume them.
topology_enumeration.why_no_single_location_suffices: Cleanup-only fixes do not align output framing or negative controls, and storage-only fixes do not handle replay ordering.

topology_enumeration.summary: Repair by rebuilding observation generation around artifact provenance while preserving runtime continuity.
topology_enumeration.why_no_single_location_suffices: Observation-only repair cannot guarantee canonical state; main-only repair cannot pass provenance and idempotence checks.

symbol_table.signature: def fold_units(items: list[object], mark: str | None = None) -> dict[str, object]
symbol_table.purpose: Combines collected records into the intermediate map used by the harness.

symbol_table.signature: def advance_span(batch: list[object], marker: str | None = None) -> list[dict[str, object]]
symbol_table.purpose: Builds the next operation sequence from a batch of local records.

symbol_table.signature: def bind_entry(slot: str, payload: dict[str, object]) -> dict[str, object]
symbol_table.purpose: Loads and stores structured local entries used by the runner.

symbol_table.signature: def shape_rows(label: str, frame: dict[str, object], order: list[str]) -> list[dict[str, object]]
symbol_table.purpose: Converts intermediate maps into externally visible observation rows.

decoy_manifest.non_fix_purpose: Writes diagnostic snapshots for operator inspection outside verifier-facing observations.
Performs sanity checks for local fixture rows before execution begins.
Prints operator-facing dry-run traces for manual inspection.

renames_during_drafting.reason: path token overlapped with instruction noun migration/state and would increase grep-collapse risk.
symbol overlapped with instruction noun replay and revealed the phase under diagnosis.
test name overlapped with instruction nouns replay and cleanup.
symbol overlapped with instruction noun observation.

difficulty_mechanism_plan.placement: runner scenarios perform restore, replay, cleanup, deletion, and rerun sequences before observations are stable.
difficulty_mechanism_plan.why_model_misses_it: A model can stop at the first healthy run and miss drift that appears only after later sequences.
difficulty_mechanism_plan.fairness_guardrail: All sequences are deterministic and reproducible through the public command and local fixtures.

difficulty_mechanism_plan.placement: initial health records and reconstructed summaries look internally consistent while later records expose divergence.
difficulty_mechanism_plan.why_model_misses_it: Surface checks encourage premature closure around the wrong authority.
difficulty_mechanism_plan.fairness_guardrail: The final verifier derives invariants from full observation traces, not hidden facts.

difficulty_mechanism_plan.placement: cleanup and rerun behavior must be safe after output deletion and repeated recovery.
difficulty_mechanism_plan.why_model_misses_it: Narrow fixes often pass one replay but fail idempotence or negative controls.
difficulty_mechanism_plan.fairness_guardrail: The public prompt names idempotent recovery and regeneration requirements.

difficulty_mechanism_plan.placement: Python source, JSON fixtures, visible docs, emitted observations, and construction manifest must agree.
difficulty_mechanism_plan.why_model_misses_it: Single-file fixes leave fixture semantics, docs, or report framing inconsistent.
difficulty_mechanism_plan.fairness_guardrail: Every externally tested schema and artifact rule has a visible home.

difficulty_mechanism_plan.placement: the solver must run and compare generated traces across modes to isolate the durable drift.
difficulty_mechanism_plan.why_model_misses_it: Static inspection alone may identify several plausible authorities that all look locally consistent.
difficulty_mechanism_plan.fairness_guardrail: No timing, latency, network, or nondeterministic crash injection is required.

calibration_plan.human_sanity: One careful human can solve using bundled source, fixtures, docs, public command, and regenerated observations in a few hours.
calibration_plan.shortcut_audit: Try static JSON writes, runner-only patches, broad state clearing, summary-only trust, test edits/deletions, and direct reward writes.
calibration_plan.ablation_plan: Remove one layer at a time: deceptive first health, cleanup rerun, durable artifact provenance, and cross-format schema checks; each removal should make a distinct shortcut viable.

pass_rate_target.basis: stronger target-agent trials on the finalized single-container task.

metrics.criterion: regenerated observations satisfy all recovery, replay, cleanup, and convergence checks.
derived provenance, generation continuity, digest, and idempotence invariants hold across scenarios.
reruns after deletion and cleanup are deterministic and do not leave stale mutable state.
public command, output path, JSON schema, and local-only runtime contract remain intact.
all required files and executable scripts are present with no forbidden packaged artifacts.

verifier_scoring_plan.binary_threshold_rule: reward.txt is 1 only when every semantic and static verifier check passes; partial metric weights are for author calibration.

subtype_milestone_plan.sequential_dependency: single-step task; sequential dependency is encoded inside deterministic recovery scenarios rather than milestone folders.
subtype_milestone_plan.sidecar_or_protocol_notes: all sidecar-like inputs are local JSON/docs fixtures under environment; no external service, database, UI, or runtime web access.

task_files.role: local input scenarios and negative controls
visible product documentation for schema and recovery guarantees

fix_frontier.distribution: one core combiner, one operation sequencer, one durable entry handler, and one observation emitter across distinct package roots; no location controls more than 25% of tests.
fix_frontier.naming_policy: Fix-path symbols use neutral engineering names and avoid every extracted instruction noun; tests use neutral numeric IDs.
fix_frontier.helpers_policy: Co-resident helpers and decoys perform genuine diagnostics or sanity checks; no cosmetic decoys or detector-padding files.

forbidden_assertion_styles: tests that only check JSON shape

task_shape.hardness_source: diagnosis plus coordinated repair across local recovery state and regenerated artifacts
task_shape.collapse_risk: naming the restore boundary or trusted artifact too directly could reduce the task to replacing one source in one emitter.

category_profile.challenge_family: live recovery replay boundary drift with durable artifact authority mismatch
category_profile.bug_family: live recovery replay boundary drift with durable artifact authority mismatch
category_profile.allowed_instruction_disclosures: Crash/restart or recovery workflow, durability guarantee, recovery command, regenerated output path, report schema, externally visible post-recovery state, and idempotent rerun expectations.

category_profile.coverage_role: Adds a state recovery crash-consistency profile task using migration-style replay drift, complementing the promoted config-policy reference rather than duplicating it.
